source_note: Snapshot of selected public work packages from community.openproject.org API (OpenProject Community instance).

items.subject: Delete project via project settings by non admins
items.description_markdown:
**As** a project admin  
**I want** to delete my project myself  
**so that** I don’t need to ask the admin to do that.

**Acceptance criteria**

*   Delete link in project settings
*   Deletion only possible for users having the “delete\_project” permission
*   Deletion possible through APIv3
*   In case subprojects are present:
    *   If the user has the “delete\_project” permission in all of the subprojects, the user is notified (a statement listing all the projects that are going to be deleted) and asked again whether he wants to delete all the projects
    *   In case the user lacks the “delete\_project” permission in any of the subprojects, the user is notified that a deletion is currently impossible as he is lacking permissions in one of the subprojects
*   “manager” role in default data receives “delete\_project” permission by default

**Wireframes**

*   See attachments

items.subject: Show meetings in time tracking calendar and project calendar
items.description_markdown:
**As** an OpenProject user  
**I want** to see my meetings also in the calendar views (project calendar, time tracking calendar)  
**so that** I can better plan my week and I can better find the meetings.

**Open**

*   [ ] Meeting quick filter
*   [ ] Meeting create action
*   [ ] Meeting preview/hover card

items.subject: Dynamic Related Work Packages
items.description_markdown:
I’d like to see work package relations be able to be defined with a query so that they are automatically updated as new tasks are added or updated to fit the criteria of the query. I’ve started tracking event type tasks and those events require keeping up with multiples of different types of tasks that meet the same criteria. Right now I have to daily go in and query to find tasks meeting that criteria and manually adding them to the task as a related work package.

Having the query defined with the WP to dynamically populate the related work package list would be most excellent.

items.subject: Bidirectional Forum - WP association
items.description_markdown: I’d like to see a way to link a Forum discussion to a work package such that when viewing the forum you can quickly see the status of the work package (perhaps tagged in the subject line with a link to the WP). In addition I’d like to have the forum discussion embedded in the work package such that when I’m viewing a work package with a related forum discussion I can easily expand the discussion to view and reply in line if needed.

items.subject: Filter work packages between two numeric values
items.description_markdown:
When specifying the work package query filters, we need the ability to either add the same field twice, once for \<= and once for \>= or have another filter option that allows you to specify something between two values.

For example, if a Custom Field of type float has values 1.4, 2.0, 2.5, 2.7, 3.0 you should be able to apply a filter that only shows 2.0, 2.5 and 2.7.

items.subject: Move Work Package should allow setting of Custom Fields
items.description_markdown:
When moving a work package to another project and type, custom fields for the selected destination should be shown and be able to be completed by the user.

Currently if there are any required custom fields in the destination project and type, the work package cannot be moved and an error message is returned.

items.subject: Global Forums View
items.description_markdown:
There should be a top level Forums page that brings in the forums from the entire project tree into a single page shown as a tree layout.

\`- Project A

*   forum 1
    
*   forum 2
    
*   Project B
    
    *   forum 1
        
*   Project C
    
    *   forum 1
        
    *   forum 2 \`

items.subject: Moving Forum Messages Across Project Boundaries
items.description_markdown: We’re getting a large number of forum messages posted to the wrong project and would like to move them to the correct location. There is already the capability to move to another forum within the same project but it appears that you can’t move it to a forum in another project. The move function should allow for moving to any forum on any project which the user has rights to see.
